message.text: S3 bucket logs: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.
S3 bucket logs: name suggests audit/compliance use AND no aws_s3_bucket_object_lock_configuration references it. Without object-lock, audit data can be modified/deleted before retention expires.

S3 bucket (logs) has no aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration. Without lifecycle rules, objects accumulate forever: old versions from versioning, abandoned multipart uploads, and stale data all bill at $0.023/GB-month with no bound.
Messages published to this topic disappear silently — no subscriber receives them.

aws_iam_user admin declared. Each IAM user is a long-lived credential surface. Legitimate cases: external system integrations that don't support IRSA / OIDC federation. Humans should go through Identity Center; pods should use IRSA; CI/CD should use OIDC.
